Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Best ISKCON centre

Social · asked by user [] · 2008-07-23 · 6 answers
Which ISKCON temple will be the best to live in. My situation is I am a Grihastha with 2 kids ages 6 and 4. So there should be preferably some kind of Krsna Conscious school, good relationships among devotees (I would like to live with people who help each other in difficult times). My wife also needs association of Devotee Matajis. Considering all these requirements which ISKCON centre will be good to move in or we can live nearby that centre.
user [75] · 2008-07-25
as it is, this question is impossible to answer, i think. you dont even state which part of the world you are living in, or where you would like to live.

potentially all temples with grhastha communities could qualify for your requirements, at least all temples with some schooling facilities nearby.

what one person considers "good relationships" may be completely unacceptable to another. devotees come from all types of backgrounds, carrying over all types of different traits into their devotional life--until theyre free from all material tinges.

ive experienced very different atmospheres in different temples, and always at least some of the devotees wanted things to be that way.

from your question, and what you say about yourself in your profile (nil), its impossible to guess what type of surrounding you might consider conducive.
user [154] · 2008-07-25
NY 26th second avenue in 1966 had some great prasadam in the summer...
user [192] · 2008-07-25
...look for a Temple with a Temple President an a fullly functioning Temple Board,there should be regular harinam and book distribution should be a priority there.
Temples that do not make devotees and only take money from the Indian congregation should be avoided.
user [38] · 2008-07-25
Subjectively speaking, the most inspiring temple of those I had a chance to visit in US, Europe and India in past 16 years was in Ljubljana, Slovenia. Not sure about gurukula there. Second was probably the Hungarian farm, then Prabhupadadesh, Italy.
user [198] · 2008-07-25
Thank you everyone for your inputs. Your answers made things a bit clear for me. It helped me to narrow down my search for that particular place.
user [38] · 2008-07-25
I was travelling around US in 1992 and have nice memories of devotess in Chicago, San Diego, Tucson (AZ), Houston (TX) and Potomac (D.C.).
